TYPUS TYPORUM , or a table of the several type , title and attribute of JESVS Christ accord to the nature of the thing themselves , as in the former treatise , they be set down after the order of the alphabet . advertisement anent the scope , use and form of this table etc i . the use of this table explain in the former song , be manifold , as first for information , to let we see these matchless and incomparable excellency that be in Jesus Christ , our lord . 2. for consolation : the heart of a sincere Christian can but be fill with ravish and transport joy when it behold such excellency in Christ ; and then begin to find a title and interest in they . 3. for spiritual devotion , every type and title almost afford excellent compe••ations to Christ , ground of confidence to draw near , matter of prayer and praise , as for example ; one in pray may say , lord thou who be the king of king , subdue my rebellious spirit to thy self ▪ by the sceptre of thy word and spirit etc that i may be of the number of thy willing people : preserve i powerful from my spiritual enemy etc thou who art that vigilant shepherd of soul , protect i from the roar lion Satan who be ready to rear i , and lead i to the live water etc 4. for matter of meditation : every type or title etc afford heavenly matter for the exercise of our spiritual thought . ii as to the exactness of it , it be easy to make it more scholastic and reduce it all to dichotomy , but i think this sufficient for the most of ordinary Christians . here follow the table . Satan be say , act 5. 3. to fill the heart to lie . why have Satan fill thy heart to lie to the holy ghost ? speak liar , when thou speak a lie , who language be this ? it|be the devil mother-tongue : whenever thou have a lie in thy mouth , it|be a sign the devil be in thy heart . but who child be thou the while ? none of God , he be the god of truth : it|be sure thou be the devil child , for he be a liar , and the father of it . o friend , be it nothing to be the devil child ? and to have thy own tongue proclaim it ? it will not be long , if thou use this trade , ere thou be carry down to thy father house , the place of liar , rev. 21. 8. 3. consider the reward of a liar even among man , which be not to be believe , when he speak the truth . common experience show , that one be once find in a lie , be hardly after believe , but be scorn as a man of no credit . 4. as this sin be hateful in it self unto God , so it make the practiser thereof to be odious and abominable unto he . Prov. 6. 16. these thing do the lord hate , yea , they be abomination unto he , a proud look , a lie tongue , etc though God hate and abhor all sin , yet some be more abominable unto he than other , among which lie be one . and Prov. 12. 22. lie lip be abomination to the lord . O young man , how shall the consideration of these thing effectual prevail with you to keep a strict watch over your self , that you break not forth into this sin of lie , but as the apostle exhort , Eph. 4. 25. put away lie speak every man truth with his neighbour . Obj. some object , and say , there be no live , or at least , no thrive , without lie . to tie our self always to speak the truth , must needs be a great hindrance to our profit . A. that profit thou get by lie , will be little profitable unto thou . for thereby thou sell thy soul unto the devil ; and that be a miserable bargain , where the soul be give in to boot for some trifle gain , which can be ransom with the whole world . Men common think , that that gain be sweet , and come easy in , that be get by lie : but alas , what sweetness or comfort can there be in that gain , that bring god curse with it ? will thy gain ( if it be the gain of the whole world ) be able to countervail the loss of God favour , and thy own salvation ? O young man , think on these thing : and whatever thy trade be , have nothing to do with the trade of lie . now that thou be set up thy trade , let not lie , ( which have a great stock go in almost every trade ) have any thing in thou . resolve to be true and honest ; content thy self with righteous gain . but if thou will yet be a liar , never henceforth count that God , but the devil be thy father : and know , that though thou lie unto man , yet God will not lie unto thou : he that be true in his promise , will be true in his threaten . God have say , liar shall be shut out of his kingdom , and shut up in the lake of fire : God will not lie unto thou . if this be thy practice , hell will be thy place , and thy portion , as sure as God be true .

his father also be a man advance unto great dignity and authority , among the Swisser in regard of his approve and well deserve part . he by daily observation , ( without doubt to his great comfort ) see and perceive more than a ordinary towardliness in his son ; and have spend here some few year , he return again to Basil , where he first begin to employ that talon which God have bestow on he ; for here he begin first to teach other that which he himself have learn ; for that peace which some so great urge and press , be open war and not peace , and that war which we be to undertake be peace , and not open war : for we do not thirst after any man blood , neither do we desire to spill it by such tu•multuous action ;
